pattern: A number is divisible by its own digit sum exactly when that digit sum divides it evenly, so computing the digit total and testing one modulo answers the question.
hints: First reduce the number to a single quantity: the total of its individual decimal digits.
Compare the original value against that digit total using a divisibility test.
If the value is a multiple of its digit sum, the digit sum is the answer; otherwise report the failure sentinel.

edge_inputs.description: single digit numbers are always divisible by themselves
digit sum does not divide the number
value just above a Harshad number

stress.description: sweep across the full small domain so both Harshad and non-Harshad outcomes appear
stress.note: domain is tiny so this just samples valid inputs
